name: Msue- Macomb County (Warren)
description_short: MSU Extension's MI Money Health provides free, university-backed financial education and counseling across Michigan, including homeownership, foreclosure prevention, and money management through certified advisors.
description_long:
MI Money Health is part of Michigan State University Extension, a publicly-funded educational program dedicated to increasing financial literacy across Michigan. The program operates with a mission to ensure every Michigan resident has access to personal finance and homeownership education regardless of financial status. Rather than selling financial products, MSU Extension functions as an educational institution providing trusted, unbiased guidance on complex financial topics.

The organization offers comprehensive financial education through multiple delivery methods: online and in-person classes, self-paced learning modules, one-time community presentations, and direct access to certified housing and money management counselors. Specific program areas include foreclosure assistance and prevention, homeownership education seminars, money management training, retirement planning resources, and specialized programs like "Rent Smart" (tenant preparation) and "Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?" (estate planning sensitivity). They host regular webinars on topics like mortgage foreclosure basics and offer youth-focused financial education through programs like Smart Money Missions.

What distinguishes MI Money Health is its university affiliation and certification standards. All counselors delivering education are certified housing and money management professionals, ensuring quality and consistency. The program publishes impact reports tracking educational outcomes and maintains a broad resource library including articles, videos, publications, and courses accessible online. Their goal is demystifying financial concepts and making best practices understandable to diverse audiences, not pushing products or services.

As a free educational provider rather than a direct service company, MI Money Health's primary limitation is that they cannot directly resolve financial problems—they educate and counsel rather than lend, repair credit, or negotiate debt settlements. While comprehensive in scope, accessing their services typically requires proactive outreach or attending scheduled events. The organization is strongest as a preventative and educational resource rather than crisis intervention for those in immediate financial distress.
pros: Certified housing and money management counselors deliver unbiased, university-backed education
Multiple delivery formats including online classes, in-person seminars, webinars, and self-paced learning for accessibility
Covers comprehensive topics from basic money management to foreclosure prevention, homeownership, and retirement planning
Free services available to all Michigan residents regardless of financial status or credit history
Publishes annual impact reports showing measurable outcomes of their financial education programs
Offers specialized programs addressing unique needs like tenant preparation and family estate planning
Available statewide with in-person programs in multiple locations including Macomb County
cons: Does not provide direct financial services—cannot lend money, repair credit, or negotiate debts
Relies on participant initiative to find and register for programs; no outreach-based case management
Limited emergency assistance for those in immediate financial crisis; primarily preventative/educational
Virtual and in-person event schedules may not align with all consumers' availability
As an educational provider, cannot provide legal advice on bankruptcy or complex financial matters
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking education on the home purchase process and mortgage basics
Homeowners facing foreclosure or financial stress who need prevention strategies and options counseling
Michigan residents wanting to build foundational money management skills and financial literacy
Renters preparing for their first lease or seeking to understand tenant responsibilities and rights
diagnosis: MI Money Health is best for Michigan residents seeking free, credible financial education from university-certified professionals—especially those preparing for homeownership or facing foreclosure. The primary caveat is that as an educational rather than service-based provider, they cannot directly resolve financial crises, handle credit repair, or provide immediate cash assistance; they serve as a preventative resource and knowledge builder rather than crisis intervention.
services: Free homeownership education seminars (in-person and virtual)
Money management education and financial literacy classes
Direct consultation with certified financial and housing counselors
Self-paced online learning modules on various financial topics
Rent Smart program covering tenant preparation and rental process
Youth financial education programs (Smart Money Missions)
Community presentations on emerging financial issues
Articles, videos, and publications on consumer finance topics
Webinars on specific topics like mortgage foreclosure basics
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
